title: For A Trafalgar Cenotaph
content:
Lover of England, stand awhile and gaze
    With thankful heart, and lips refrained from praise;
    They rest beyond the speech of human pride
    Who served with Nelson and with Nelson died.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Henry John Newbolt, Sir delivers a powerful performance in "For A Trafalgar Cenotaph"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
